workflow_rationale: This is a refactor workflow because we're consolidating duplicate code (jail-base role) and removing orphaned files without changing functionality. The pattern is: enhance shared role → migrate consumers → remove duplicates → cleanup.

phases.name: Enhance Shared Role
phases.description: Add NAT networking features from semaphore/roles/jail-base to shared/roles/jail-base

subtasks.description: Add network_mode variables to shared jail-base defaults

verification.instructions: Verify shared/roles/jail-base/defaults/main.yml contains jail_network_mode and jail_nat_interface variables

subtasks.notes: Add the network_mode and nat_interface variables that exist in semaphore version

subtasks.description: Update jail-config.conf.j2 template to support NAT mode

verification.instructions: Verify template has conditional logic for jail_network_mode (alias vs nat)

subtasks.notes: Update interface configuration to use jail_nat_interface when network_mode is 'nat'

subtasks.description: Update rc.conf.j2 template to support NAT mode

verification.instructions: Verify template has conditional logic for jail_network_mode affecting network config

subtasks.notes: Add conditional network configuration based on jail_network_mode

subtasks.description: Copy pf.conf.j2 template to shared role
subtasks.notes: This template is needed for NAT networking mode

subtasks.description: Remove become: false line from shared jail-base

verification.instructions: Verify line 121 'become: false' is removed from Wait for jail task

subtasks.notes: This line exists in shared version but not semaphore version - likely unnecessary

phases.name: Verify Enhanced Role
phases.description: Test that the enhanced shared role works correctly

subtasks.description: Verify ansible syntax for enhanced role
subtasks.notes: Syntax check ensures YAML is valid after modifications

phases.name: Remove Duplicate Role
phases.description: Remove the duplicate jail-base role from semaphore/roles/

subtasks.notes: Delete the entire semaphore/roles/jail-base directory since shared version now has all features

subtasks.description: Verify semaphore still uses shared jail-base role
subtasks.notes: Ensure playbooks still work after removing duplicate role

phases.name: Remove Orphaned Files
phases.description: Remove orphaned test files and backups

subtasks.notes: This is a test/debug playbook not needed in production
This appears to be a test playbook that duplicates test-vars.yml

phases.name: Final Integration Test
phases.description: Run full CI test suite to verify all changes work together

subtasks.description: Verify Ansible syntax across all playbooks

verification.command: cd semaphore && for pb in site.yml playbooks/*.yml; do ansible-playbook --syntax-check "$pb" || exit 1; done && echo 'All playbooks valid'

subtasks.notes: Comprehensive syntax check of all playbooks

verification.instructions: Review changes summary and verify acceptance criteria are met

subtasks.notes: Final documentation of what was changed and why

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution required - refactor workflow with dependencies
parallelism.rationale: Refactor workflows require sequential execution to safely migrate from old to new implementation

acceptance_criteria: Only one jail-base role exists at shared/roles/jail-base/
All Ansible playbooks pass syntax check
Shared jail-base role supports both alias and NAT networking modes
verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk refactor - changes shared infrastructure role but preserves functionality. CI syntax checks sufficient for validation.

commands: cd semaphore && for pb in playbooks/*.yml; do ansible-playbook --syntax-check "$pb"; done

checks: Verify shared/roles/jail-base contains all files from semaphore version
Verify orphaned files are removed (test-vars.yml, backup-new.yml)
Review git diff to ensure only expected files were modified
